Scenario.title: Operation Dust Storm: Cyber Attack on Transportation Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Intermediate A sophisticated yet intermediate-level cyber attack targeting transportation systems through spear-phishing campaigns, malware execution, and privilege escalation to disrupt critical infrastructure operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disrupt transportation networks to create chaos and economic impact

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Social engineering", "Vulnerability", "Improper configuration", "Web server services such as emails and databases", "Legacy systems"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-35082 [CWE-XXX]: Suggested exploit for spear-phishing campaigns
stage.description: Threat actors send spear-phishing emails to transportation staff containing malicious attachments (e.g., fake delivery manifests) designed to trick recipients into enabling macros, thereby establishing initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2022-35405 [CWE-XXX]: Exploit for deceptive filenames in malicious documents
stage.description: A macro-enabled Microsoft Word document is executed, deploying a downloader payload that establishes persistence and connects to a remote command-and-control server.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A hidden scheduled task is created to periodically download and execute C2 payloads, ensuring continuous access to the compromised system despite potential detection efforts.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Unpatched legacy SCADA systems are exploited to escalate privileges, granting attackers administrative control over critical infrastructure components like traffic signal controllers.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.description: Attackers employ basic encryption techniques to mask C2 communication traffic, evading detection by network monitoring tools that rely on signature-based analysis.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Compromised systems manipulate traffic signal timing or disable safety protocols, causing operational disruptions, delays, or potential collisions in rail and road networks.

Scenario.summary: Operation Dust Storm leverages phishing, malware, and legacy system vulnerabilities to disrupt transportation infrastructure, demonstrating how basic adversaries can exploit human and technical weaknesses to achieve significant operational impact.
